description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed channels. The block has a solid, monolithic appearance with sharp, clean edges and a uniform thickness. The stone surface is dark gray with a slightly mottled texture, suggesting a granite or basalt material. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. This type of block is commonly used as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments, such as on sidewalks, plazas, or as part of a traffic control system.
description: A dark gray, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The block is solid, with smooth sides and a textured, durable appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the striped, grooved top surface and the solid, smooth front face.
description_view_1: Side view emphasizing the depth and regularity of the grooves, with the block's width clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grooved top and the plain, solid back face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the parallel grooves and the block's substantial width.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained, with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
